sections.paragraph:
ἀλαζονεύομαι (ἀλαζών; Aristoph., Lysias et al.; Philo, Fug. 33; Jos., Bell. 4, 122) boast, be boastful w. acc. about someth. (Aeschin. 3, 218; Herodian 2, 7, 2 χρήματα; Wsd 2:16) μηδέν 1 Cl 2:1. τὴν μείωσιν τῆς σαρκός Dg 4:4. Abs. (Timaeus Hist. [IV–III b.c.]: 566 Fgm. 132 Jac.; Dio Chrys. 26 [43], 2) 1 Cl 38:2.

Aristoph Aristoph , V–IV b.c.—List 5

Lysias Lysias , V–IV b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

acc. acc. = accusative

someth. someth. = something

Aeschin Aeschin , IV b.c.; spurious letters II a.d.—List 5

Herodian Herodian = Herodianus Historicus, III a.d.—List 5

1 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

Dg Dg = Letter of Diognetus, attributed to an unidentifiable apologist; II a.d.—List 1

Timaeus Timaeus , IV–III b.c.—List 5

Hist.

Hist. (after a personal name)=historicus, historian

hist. = historian, historical

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

Jac. Jac. = Die Fragmente der griechischen Historiker, ed. FJacoby—Lists 5, 6

Dio Chrys Dio Chrys , I–II a.d.—List 5

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 40
